chunks.text: Quantum Advantages via Fourier Growth Analysis of Boolean Functions | EECS at UC Berkeley Quantum Advantages via Fourier Growth Analysis of Boolean Functions Kewen Wu EECS Department, University of California, Berkeley Technical Report No. UCB/EECS-2025-39 May 1, 2025 http://www2.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2025/EECS-2025-39.pdf Intuitively quantum computations can easily aggregate signals on the Fourier basis whereas classical algorithms cannot. Raz-Tal (STOC'19, JACM'22) and Bansal-Sinha (STOC'21) formalized this using the notion of Fourier growth from Boolean function analysis. Continuing this line of work, we establish sharp Fourier growth bounds for classical (parity) query algorithms, randomized communication protocols, and parallel quantum query algorithms with limited adaptivity. As such, we obtain unconditional exponential quantum advantages in various query and communication settings. Raz-Tal (STOC'19, JACM'22) and Bansal-Sinha (STOC'21) formalized this using the notion of Fourier growth from Boolean function analysis.
